# Codex Rehome

Codex Rehome is an open-source Codex skill and script toolkit for migrating OpenAI Codex Desktop between macOS and Windows computers, or preserving Codex before reinstalling the same computer's operating system.

Repository: https://github.com/CalebYcj/codex-rehome

## Summary

Use this project to package, transfer, restore, and verify Codex Desktop conversations, sessions, memories, skills, plugins, MCP/connectors, generated images, project folders, and path mappings when moving Codex between computers or reinstalling the OS on the same computer.

Core model: restoring visible project conversations has four layers, not one. The file layer copies sessions, `session_index.jsonl`, skills, plugins, SQLite state, generated artifacts, and project folders. The path mapping layer rewrites source paths to target paths in SQLite and session JSONL metadata. The index layer makes conversations discoverable through `state_*.sqlite.threads`, `rollout_path`, `cwd`, titles, timestamps, and `session_index.jsonl`. The app registration layer makes Codex Desktop register/open the restored workspace through its own official entry point.

Do not treat UI project recovery as manual JSON/SQLite patching. On Mac, the observed durable app-visible registration command is `/Applications/Codex.app/Contents/Resources/codex app <restored-project-path>`. Windows should use the same class of official open-workspace operation, such as `codex app <restored-project-path>` or the equivalent Codex Desktop mechanism.

Plain user flow: old computer/source AI packages Codex data and chosen project folders into a private zip; the user transfers the zip privately; new computer/target AI restores the zip after Codex is installed and logged in, maps paths, merges indexes, restores projects, registers/opens projects in Codex Desktop, and runs verification. When explaining this project to a non-technical user, first say which stage they are in and what action is needed next.

Same-computer reinstall flow: before wiping Windows, source AI must create the package on a non-system partition or external disk, not Desktop, Downloads, `%USERPROFILE%`, or any C: path that will be erased. Use the Windows package script with `-Out "D:\Codex-Rehome-Backup"` or another surviving location, save the GitHub URL/README screenshot/restore prompt next to the zip, verify zip + manifests + checksums exist there, then reinstall. After reinstall, install and log in to Codex, give the zip and saved prompt to the new system's Codex, restore, register projects, and verify.

Supported migration directions:

- Mac to Windows: supported.
- Windows to Mac: supported.
- Windows to Windows: supported.
- Mac to Mac: supported.
- Same Windows computer OS reinstall: supported when the package is saved to a surviving non-system partition or external disk before reinstall.

## Primary use cases

- Migrate OpenAI Codex Desktop from Mac to Windows.
- Migrate OpenAI Codex Desktop from Windows to Mac.
- Move Codex Desktop from one Windows computer to another Windows computer.
- Move Codex Desktop from one Mac to another Mac.
- Preserve Codex before reinstalling Windows on the same computer, then restore after reinstall.
- Back up Codex conversations and JSONL sessions.
- Restore Codex memories, skills, plugins, and generated images.
- Hand off a local AI agent workspace to another computer.
- Preserve project context without moving secrets by default.

## Script map

- Package on Mac: `codex-rehome/scripts/create_mac_codex_migration_package.sh`
- Package on Windows: `codex-rehome/scripts/create_windows_codex_migration_package.ps1`
- Restore to Mac: `Restore-Codex-To-Mac.sh --restore-projects` in the generated package, or `scripts/restore_codex_to_mac.sh --restore-projects`
- Restore to Windows: `Restore-Codex-To-Windows.ps1` in the generated package, or `scripts/restore_codex_to_windows.ps1`
- Verify Mac restore: `Verify-Codex-Mac-Restore.sh --json`
- Verify Windows restore: `Verify-Codex-Windows-Restore.ps1`

## Safety

Standard mode excludes auth tokens, browser cookies, Login Data, Local Storage, `.env` files, private keys, sockets, `.git`, `node_modules`, virtual environments, and runtime caches. The target computer should log in again manually.

Windows packages use package schema version 3, forward-slash zip entries, LF/no-BOM `SHA256SUMS.txt`, `MANIFEST.txt`, and `MANIFEST.json`. Windows packaging creates or repairs `home/.codex/session_index.jsonl` when needed and exports `metadata/thread_index_export.json`, `metadata/path_map.json`, `metadata/selected_chats.json`, and `metadata/project_ui_registry_export.json`. On Mac, restore normalizes package permissions, fails if `home/.codex` is missing, and can restore projects to `~/Documents/Codex-Restored-Projects` with `--restore-projects`.

Restore scripts default to merge restore. They preserve target login/config identity files and do not overwrite `state_*.sqlite`, `memories_*.sqlite`, or `goals_*.sqlite` unless `--replace-state` or `-ReplaceState` is passed. Whole-home replacement requires `--replace-codex-home` or `-ReplaceCodexHome`.

Windows packages can include highlighted session JSONL files with `-SelectedChat`. These are copied to `selected_chats/`, restored into `home/.codex/sessions` when needed, and indexed in `session_index.jsonl`; the Mac verifier reports selected chat readiness across sessions, session index, SQLite threads, rollout paths, Mac cwd/path mapping, session JSONL metadata, old source path removal, global project registry, and Codex app project registration. On Mac, restored project folders must be registered with `/Applications/Codex.app/Contents/Resources/codex app <restored-project-path>`; the restore script invokes this automatically after `--restore-projects`.

Do not rewrite old Codex session JSONL files in bulk. Preserve old paths in history and reopen matching project folders on the target computer.

Known limits: this project is not official cloud sync and cannot guarantee that every old thread keeps a live working-directory handle after cross-OS path changes. Restored conversations are best treated as historical context; continue work by reopening the restored project folder in Codex and starting or continuing from the target project's registered workspace. Project source files are only included when explicitly passed with `--project` or `-Project`. App sidebar visibility requires Codex Desktop project registration, not file copy alone. Login state, browser sessions, secrets, `.env`, private keys, cookies, `.git`, `node_modules`, virtualenvs, running terminals, unsaved buffers, in-memory state, and GUI layout are not migrated by default. Native dependencies and OS-specific runtimes should be reinstalled or rebuilt on the target computer. Cross-account or cross-workspace restores may show local history but still require fresh remote authorization.

See feature status for supported directions, default exclusions, and target verification steps:

- https://github.com/CalebYcj/codex-rehome/blob/main/docs/validation-status.md
